landing.testimonial2Text: Bank reconciliation and forecasting let us cut our closing time from 5 days to 2.

landing.faq1Q: Is Atlas FnA compliant with SYSCOHADA revised 2017?
landing.faq1A: Yes, 100%. Natively embedded OHADA chart of accounts, financial statements matching the official templates, and an audit trail meeting OHADA requirements.
landing.faq2Q: Can I try it before paying?
landing.faq2A: Subscribe now with all features included. No credit card required.
landing.faq3Q: Which payment methods do you accept?
landing.faq3A: Mobile Money (Orange Money, MTN MoMo, Wave), bank transfer, credit card (Visa, Mastercard). Billing in FCFA or EUR.
landing.faq4A: AES-256 encryption, daily backup, SHA-256 audit trail. GDPR and OHADA regulatory compliance.
landing.faq5Q: Can I migrate from another software?
landing.faq5A: CSV/Excel import of your journal entries, chart of accounts, third parties and fixed assets. Assisted migration available.
landing.faq6A: The desktop app works 100% offline with automatic synchronization once the connection is back.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Join 500+ companies that trust Atlas FnA. Subscribe now.

aiConfig.pageSubtitle: Management of artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms
aiConfig.lstmDesc: This model uses an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) neural network to analyse historical cash flows and predict future movements with high accuracy. The algorithm takes seasonal trends, customer payment cycles and spending patterns into account.
aiConfig.isolationForestDesc: This algorithm uses the Isolation Forest method to detect unusual transactions that may indicate errors, fraud or abnormal behaviour. It continuously learns normal patterns in order to better identify anomalies.
aiConfig.randomForestDesc: This model uses an ensemble of decision trees (Random Forest) to suggest the appropriate accounts and propose automatic journal entries based on the analysis of thousands of similar past transactions.
aiConfig.gradientBoostingDesc: This Gradient Boosting algorithm analyses customer payment behaviour to predict default risk. It combines several weak models to produce a robust and reliable customer risk prediction.
aiConfig.dbscanDesc: DBSCAN clustering algorithm used to identify customer behaviour segments and detect abnormal behaviour. It automatically detects anomalies as noise within the clusters.
aiConfig.prophetDesc: Facebook's Prophet model for time-series forecasting and detection of seasonal anomalies in cash flows. It accounts for trends, seasonality and public holidays.
aiConfig.networkAnalysisDesc: Transaction network analysis to detect suspicious circular flows, abnormal hubs and money-laundering patterns. It uses graph theory to identify abnormal structures.
aiConfig.syscohadaDesc: Hybrid system combining SYSCOHADA business rules and machine learning to ensure accounting, tax and regulatory compliance with OHADA standards (WAEMU/CEMAC).
aiConfig.ruleRs002Desc: Check of VAT rate (18%) and regimes
aiConfig.provisionScaleDesc: 25% at 3 months, 50% at 6 months, 100% at 1 year
aiConfig.ruleRs010Desc: JA, JV, JT, JO, JP present
aiConfig.ruleRs014Desc: 2.5% of revenue or 1/3 of prior-year tax, quarterly
aiConfig.mlDsfAnomaliesDesc: Inconsistencies in the tax and statistical return
aiConfig.mlCfaParityDesc: Check of the 655.957 FCFA/EUR exchange rate
aiConfig.svmDesc: Support vector machine (SVM) with an RBF kernel for advanced transaction classification and automatic categorisation of accounting operations. Particularly effective at separating complex classes in high-dimensional spaces.
aiConfig.xgboostDesc: XGBoost is a gradient boosting algorithm optimised for maximum performance. It combines several weak decision trees into a powerful model, ideal for predicting payment behaviour, optimising matching recommendations and anticipating financial risks.
aiConfig.hybridApproachDesc: The Atlas FnA system intelligently combines deterministic business rules and probabilistic artificial intelligence for optimal anomaly detection and accurate recommendations.
aiConfig.deterministicRulesDesc: Immediate detection of obvious violations: accounting balance, credit limits, VAT consistency
aiConfig.probabilisticMlDesc: Identification of subtle patterns and complex anomalies through continuous learning
aiConfig.compositeScoringDesc: x2 correlation boost when rules and ML converge on the same detection
aiConfig.ruleDebitCreditBalanceDesc: Debit = Credit for every journal entry
aiConfig.ruleAutoMatchingDesc: Invoices/Payments with a net amount of 0
aiConfig.ruleConsistentVatDesc: VAT rate according to tax regime
aiConfig.ruleTransactionSplittingDesc: Multiple small amounts = one large amount
aiConfig.isoForestCardDesc: Detection of complex anomalies that rules do not capture
aiConfig.dbscanCardDesc: Behavioural segmentation of customers and suppliers
aiConfig.lstmCardDesc: Treasury forecasts and detection of temporal drifts
aiConfig.graphCardDesc: Detection of circular flows and suspicious transaction networks
aiConfig.explainabilityDesc: Every detection comes with a full justification combining business rules and ML insights.
aiConfig.violationAmountSigma: Amount > 3σ from the customer profile (+25 pts)
aiConfig.violationOffHours: Transaction outside business hours at 11pm (+15 pts)
aiConfig.mlGraphCircular: Graph Analysis: circular flow detected (+17 pts)
aiConfig.actionRequestProof: Request supporting document from the customer
aiConfig.useCaseFraudDesc: Anti-fraud rule (circular flows) + Graph Analysis ML detects a network of suspicious transactions A→B→C→A.
aiConfig.useCaseUnpaidDesc: Deadline rules (>3 late payments) + Gradient Boosting ML (historical analysis) identify a high-risk customer.
aiConfig.useCaseSmartMatchingDesc: Accounting rules (exact amount) + Random Forest ML (smart matching) optimise automatic matching.
aiConfig.asyncPipelineDesc: Parallel processing of rules + ML with intelligent merging of results
aiConfig.hybridCacheDesc: Redis for fast rules, PostgreSQL for ML history
aiConfig.correlationScoringDesc: When rules and ML detect the same anomaly, the final score is boosted (x2) because convergence strengthens confidence.
aiConfig.adaptiveThresholdsDesc: Detection thresholds adjust automatically to the context (industry, company size, seasonality) to reduce false positives.
aiConfig.feedbackLoopDesc: User validations/corrections feed both the rules (adjusted thresholds) and the ML (retraining) for continuous improvement.
aiConfig.continuousLearningDesc: Atlas FnA's AI models improve constantly through continuous learning based on your data and your feedback.
aiConfig.dataCollectionDesc: The models continuously collect new transactions, invoices and payments to enrich their learning base.
aiConfig.userFeedbackDesc: Every validation or correction you make allows the models to learn from their mistakes and improve their predictions.
aiConfig.autoRetrainingDesc: The models are automatically retrained every week with the new data to maintain optimal accuracy.
aiConfig.driftDetectionDesc: The system automatically detects performance drift and triggers retraining when needed.
aiConfig.stepDataCollectionDesc: New transactions and feedback are collected and stored in a learning buffer. Threshold: 1,000 new samples minimum.
aiConfig.stepFeaturePrepDesc: Raw data is transformed into features usable by the algorithms, with normalisation and encoding.
aiConfig.stepRetrainingDesc: Each model is retrained with historical data plus new data. Average duration: 2-4 hours depending on the algorithm.
aiConfig.stepValidationDesc: The new model is validated on a test set. If the metrics are better (+2% minimum), the model is deployed.
aiConfig.stepDeploymentDesc: The new model transparently replaces the previous one. The old model is kept as a backup for 30 days.

dataMigration.pageSubtitle: Import wizard from third-party software (Sage, Ciel, EBP, Odoo, FEC, Excel...). OHADA compliant · SYSCOHADA 2017 revised chart of accounts.
dataMigration.step1QuestionBefore: On which date are you cutting over to
dataMigration.step1Intro: The mode determines the scope of the files to import and the accounting take-on strategy under SYSCOHADA revised 2017.
dataMigration.mode2Desc: On 01/01/N, just after closing the previous fiscal year. Minimal take-on via the N-1 closing trial balance (opening balances).
dataMigration.mode2Detail: SYSCOHADA standard for accounting software changes. Only one file required: the trial balance as at 31/12/N-1, which automatically becomes the opening (AN) journal. Class 1-5 accounts (balance sheet) are carried forward as opening balances; classes 6-7 (income statement) are reset to zero. Third parties and fixed assets are optional to enrich the master data. Detailed journal entry history remains available in the legacy software.
dataMigration.mode1Desc: Fiscal year N has already started. Take-on via the General Ledger (opening balances + movements from 01/01/N to the cut-over date).
dataMigration.mode1Detail: Use this when changing software mid-year. Only one mandatory file: the General Ledger of the current fiscal year, containing the AN opening entries and all movements up to the cut-over date. Document numbering continues from the last number of the legacy software. The SYSCOHADA chart of accounts is already embedded — no need to import it. Third parties and fixed assets remain optional.
dataMigration.mode3Desc: The entire history of closed fiscal years is taken on in Atlas. Reserved for audit, merger or OHADA legal requirement cases.
dataMigration.mode3Detail: Heavy take-on: one General Ledger file per fiscal year to migrate + mandatory third parties and fixed assets. May represent 200,000 to 500,000 journal entries. Each migrated fiscal year generates a distinct entry in the SHA-256 audit trail (OHADA art. 24 compliance). Not recommended outside a regulatory need — prefer Mode 2 and keep the archive in the legacy software.

dataMigration.infoMode2Title: Why the trial balance and not the General Ledger?
dataMigration.infoMode2Text: The trial balance condenses everything into a single line per account (the closing balance as at 31/12/N-1). That is exactly what Atlas FnA needs to open fiscal year N — no more, no less.
dataMigration.infoMode1Text: Journal entries from January to the cut-over date exist in Sage but not yet in Atlas. Without them the trial balance would be wrong, third-party balances wrong and VAT returns impossible. Export the General Ledger filtered on fiscal year N only (exclude the AN journal).
dataMigration.infoMode3Text: One General Ledger file per fiscal year. Do not merge several fiscal years into a single file. Atlas FnA checks cross-year consistency: N-3 closing balance sheet = N-2 opening balance sheet, etc.
